title: Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (Families)
text:
Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (Families) - SupportGoWhere
Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (Families)
Share link
Provides up to $120,000 for eligible first-time applicants applying as a family to assist with new or resale flat purchases.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Up to $120,000 Enhanced CPF Housing Grant for first-timer families
The Grant can be used to offset the flat purchase price or reduce the required housing loan
Households with lower household incomes will receive larger grants
Collapse all sections
About this support

Up to $120,000 for applicants purchasing a new or resale flat as a family

Up to $60,000 for couples comprising a first-time and second-time applicant

The grant amount is based on the average gross monthly household income, with lower-income households receiving more support

The Enhanced CPF Housing Grant (EHG) can be used to:

Offset the purchase price of the flat

Reduce the housing loan required for the flat purchase

Who is this for?

Applicants forming a core family nucleus, consisting of the core applicant(s) and occupier(s)

Average gross monthly household income of $9,000 or below for first-timer households (including all applicants and occupiers)

For mixed households (first-timer and second-timer), half of the average gross monthly household income must not exceed $4,500

If you are buying a flat with your parents, you and at least 1 parent must be a Singapore Citizen, and both parents must be first-timers and meet all the eligibility conditions

Have worked continuously for at least 12 months, 2 months before the HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter application, and were employed at the time of the HFE letter application

Flat has a remaining lease of more than 20 years

To qualify for the full grant, flat must have sufficient remaining lease to cover the youngest core applicant and/or core occupier to the age of 95

Must not own, have an interest in or disposed of any private residential property (local or overseas) within the last 30 months

All persons listed in the HFE application can, as a household, own up to 1 non-residential property at HFE letter application and up to 30 months before HFE letter application

How to apply?

Obtain a HDB Flat Eligibility (HFE) letter through the HDB Flat Portal to determine your eligibility for this Grant and others

If you meet the eligibility criteria, you may apply for the grant when submitting your resale application or after you have booked a flat from HDB

The Grant will be credited to the CPF Ordinary Accounts of eligible household members

Only core applicants can use their share of the housing grants to pay for the flat purchase

Where can I find help?

View more details

HDB Hotline: 6866-3066

Submit an enquiry

Scheme last updated 22 May 2026
